candidate_rule: When the starting pitcher on the 'safe' side of an under has a documented ERA above 8.00 in multiple prior starts against the opposing lineup, treat that as a disqualifying factor for the under regardless of park factors — but validate against a larger sample before hardcoding.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule. One loss where Kirby's Padres-specific splits were the deciding factor is noise; need N≥5 similar setups to confirm the pattern.

candidate_rule: When a starter carries a WHIP above 1.45, do not lean Under regardless of the opposing ace — high-WHIP pitchers generate multi-run innings via baserunner accumulation at a rate that overwhelms even elite run suppression on the other side; flag any Under pick where one starter's WHIP exceeds 1.40 for manual review. (N=1, insufficient to establish rule — monitor for pattern.)
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule. One blowup from a high-WHIP starter does not validate a blanket filter — watch for recurrence across 10+ similar setups before codifying.

candidate_rule: When the opposing starter's current-month ERA diverges from his season ERA by 2+ runs (Junk: 4.14 season / 6.35 May), treat the under as structurally compromised and require the total to be set at least 0.5 below the implied run total before playing it — N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule. One loss on a bad-month ERA divergence is noise; track over minimum 10 comparable starts before codifying.

candidate_rule: Before backing a home favorite in a must-win elimination game, validate opponent's current playoff momentum streak and series point differential — if the road team's differential exceeds +150 over 10+ games, fade the home urgency narrative regardless of spread value; N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — one game where a dominant road team steamrolled a fatigued home side does not confirm the pattern.

candidate_rule: Before running autopsy, cross-validate result_score against an external box score source — a misattributed score from an adjacent game in the same series is a known failure mode; add a date-stamp check against the game_id or box score URL (N=1 occurrence, insufficient to establish pattern).
sample_size_warning: N=1 data-entry error observed; watching for pattern of same-series score confusion before formalizing a validation rule.

candidate_rule: Do not back a run-line favorite when the anchor starter has fewer than 40 IP in the current season AND the implied win margin requires both the starter to hold AND the opposing back-end arm to implode — two independent events that must co-occur; N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — but the failure mode (tiny-sample ERA regression + key lineup scratch) is a known structural risk that the pick's own 'case against' section identified and dismissed.

candidate_rule: When anchoring an under on a specific starter's ERA/HR suppression, cross-check whether that pitcher has a recent 'cluster' of bad starts masked by a low season ERA — Suarez had allowed 12 of his 13 earned runs in just 13 innings across a subset of starts, a red flag that the ERA was misleading about his true variance profile; flag starters whose ERA and xERA diverge by >0.75 runs for under consideration (N=1, insufficient to establish rule).
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — one Suarez blowup does not confirm a systematic ERA-masking pattern, but the pre-game data point (12 of 13 ER in 13 innings of bad starts) was available and should have widened the confidence interval on his floor.

candidate_rule: When the Cubs' (or any team's) listed starter is a same-day recall from Triple-A with a career MLB ERA above 5.00 and no MLB starts in the current season, treat the opposing half of the total as uncapped and do not play unders unless the total is set 1.5+ runs above the ace's expected contribution alone — N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule. One emergency-callup implosion is noise, but the DATA failure here (no MLB 2026 stats existed for Wicks at pick time, and the pack explicitly flagged it) is a process gap worth tracking.

candidate_rule: When a team's best win in a series came against a rested opponent returning from a long layoff (11+ days), discount that result as a talent signal and weight the subsequent games more heavily before pricing home-dog value — but N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule.

candidate_rule: When the under thesis is structurally dependent on one pitcher going deep (6+ IP, ≤2 ER), apply a 'short-start discount' — if that pitcher's start-length variance (e.g. % of starts under 5 IP) exceeds 15%, reduce confidence by at least 1 unit; N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — single data point, monitoring for pattern of over-reliance on ERA without start-length floor validation.

candidate_rule: When the named anchor starter has allowed 3+ ER in each of his prior two starts AND is projected for a rising pitch count (70+ through 4 IP in recent outings), discount under confidence by at least one tier and flag bullpen exposure as a primary risk, not a footnote — but validate across a minimum of 15–20 similar starter-fatigue under spots before codifying as a hard filter.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule; this is a directional signal only and requires pattern confirmation across a larger sample of 'ace in mini-slump' under bets.

candidate_rule: Before backing an under involving Bibee, require his last-start HR/9 and hard-hit rate to be within 1.0 of his season average — a single blowup start is a warning sign of mechanical issues that carry over; N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — monitor Bibee under picks for pattern over next 4-6 starts.

candidate_rule: When a starter's trailing-month ERA diverges significantly from their season ERA AND their last-10-start ERA is trending down, weight the recent trend over the monthly split before fading them — but validate this rule across a minimum of 20+ comparable 'ERA-rebound' starts before treating it as a systematic edge.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule; this is a single-game observation and could easily be noise.

source_loss: Ketel Marte Total Bases Over 1.5 (LAD @ ARI)
candidate_rule: Before submitting any player prop, validate that the targeted hitter faces the identified weak pitcher — not the opposing starter; a directional mismatch (hitter vs. wrong pitcher) is a disqualifying error that should void the pick pre-submission. (N=1, insufficient to establish rule.)
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — but this is a process/logic error, not a sample size question. The mismatch should be caught at pick-generation time regardless of sample.

source_loss: Bobby Witt Jr. Total Bases Over 1.5 (KC @ CIN)
candidate_rule: When a starting pitcher's ERA over his last 6 starts is more than 2 runs below his season ERA, flag the pick and re-evaluate using recent-form metrics rather than season aggregates before publishing — but apply only after N≥10 similar cases to confirm the pattern holds.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — this is a directional flag only.

candidate_rule: Before backing a starter as a repeat-matchup favorite within 7 days, cross-check career splits against that opponent — if career ERA vs. that team is 1.5+ runs above season ERA, flag as a MODEL risk and require a line of -110 or better to proceed; N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — this is a pattern to watch, not a validated filter.

candidate_rule: When the under thesis depends on a single elite starter suppressing one side, require the opposing starter's FIP to be within 1.0 of the total-implied run expectation per team — if the gap is wider, the under is structurally one-sided and the total is more likely to be driven by the weaker arm; flag as MODEL RISK before publishing. (N=1, insufficient to establish rule — monitor for pattern across similar 'ace carries the under' setups.)
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule.

candidate_rule: Do not use a team's same-round home-game scoring averages as a baseline for a new series — opponent defensive quality resets; require at least one data point from the new matchup or a head-to-head regular season pace proxy before projecting totals above the market line. (N=1, insufficient to establish rule.)
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — one game where both stars shot poorly and a bench went cold is noise, not a pattern.

candidate_rule: When a player's series-specific sample (WCF, N=7) diverges materially from their full playoff sample (N=17), weight the larger sample more heavily in the points projection — especially when the opponent's defensive scheme is structurally different from the prior series opponent; validate this rule at N≥10 before applying as a hard filter.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — this is a single-game observation and could reflect Finals debut variance as much as a systematic modeling error.

candidate_rule: Before posting any Wrigley under that leans on wind-in suppression, cross-check at least two real-time wind sources (e.g., Ballpark Pal + Weather.com game-time reading) within 2 hours of first pitch, and void the pick if any source shows wind blowing out — N=1, insufficient to establish rule, but the directional error here was the entire load-bearing assumption.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule. One wind-data miss does not confirm a systematic sourcing failure, but the magnitude of the directional error (in vs. out) warrants an immediate pre-game wind verification step in the checklist.

candidate_rule: Do not finalize a total bet when the opposing starter is unannounced or listed as TBD; require official lineup confirmation before locking the pick, especially when the starter identity is the primary driver of the thesis — N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — but the failure mode (betting a total against an unconfirmed starter) is structurally repeatable and worth flagging as a process check.

candidate_rule: Before capping an Under on a Sasaki start, require his last-7-days command metrics (BB/9, Strike%) to confirm the Angels outing wasn't an outlier — one elite start does not establish a trend; N=1 on the Ohtani-scratch-deflates-Dodgers-offense pattern, also insufficient to formalize.
sample_size_warning: N=1 loss, insufficient to establish a rule. Single-start regression is inherent pitcher variance, but the structural error — projecting forward from one career-best outing without confirming command stability — is a repeatable model mistake worth tracking.

candidate_rule: Before fading a starter on ERA alone, check road/home splits for the last 4-5 starts — if the road ERA diverges from season ERA by 2+ runs, the season number is not the operative number for this game; N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — but the split-blindness pattern is worth flagging as a recurring model vulnerability to monitor.

candidate_rule: Before betting an Over on a pitching rematch scheduled within 7 days of the first meeting, check the starter's most recent line against that specific opponent — if it diverges sharply from season ERA, weight the recent matchup line more heavily than the season number; N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — one rematch spot does not confirm a pattern.

candidate_rule: Flag any total pick where the named starting pitcher accounts for >50% of the run-suppression thesis — require a re-evaluation trigger if that SP is scratched within 3 hours of first pitch before locking the bet; N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — but the failure mode (SP scratch invalidating a total pick) is a known, recurring risk worth monitoring across the full season sample.

candidate_rule: Before backing an Under anchored to a specific starter's ERA, check rolling 3-start ERA — if a pitcher has allowed 3+ ER in each of his last 3 outings, discount the season ERA and flag as 'ERA mirage'; do not use season ERA as the primary anchor without recency adjustment. sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule. One start does not confirm ERA regression as a systematic blind spot, but the pre-game data showing three consecutive 3+ ER outings was available and unused.

candidate_rule: Before backing a run-line favorite on starter ERA mismatch, check the opposing starter's splits against that specific team — if career ERA vs. opponent diverges 2+ runs from season ERA, discount the mismatch thesis proportionally; N=1 against this specific Rogers/LAD pattern, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — one game where Rogers outperformed his season line is noise, not a pattern.

candidate_rule: When the under anchor is the *away* starter (not the home ace), require the away SP's ERA at the specific road park to be under 4.50 AND his HR/9 to be under 1.20 before counting him as an under-suppressor — N=1, insufficient to establish rule.

candidate_rule: Flag any under pick where the primary pitcher anchor has allowed 4+ ER in 2 of their last 3 starts — season ERA is a lagging stat and recent form should override it as the primary thesis driver; validate with N≥20 before hardening.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — this is a directional signal only, not a validated pattern.

candidate_rule: Before backing an Under anchored on a specific starter's ERA, cross-check his last 2 starts for hit/contact quality (xwOBAcon, hard-hit rate) — a sub-3.00 ERA can mask a pitcher who is trending toward regression; require at least 2 consecutive starts with <7 hits allowed before treating ERA as predictive. (N=1, insufficient to establish rule.)
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — one Cole implosion does not confirm a pattern, but worth flagging that TJ-return workload management may create start-to-start variance that season ERA obscures.

candidate_rule: When a starter's K/9 implies ~6 Ks per 6 IP, model the over/under on innings pitched as a second variable — a pitcher going 7+ IP inflates K totals beyond the line regardless of opponent K-rate; flag any under bet where the starter has a recent history of deep outings (N=1 here, insufficient to establish rule).
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — one dominant outing does not invalidate the contact-suppression thesis, but the innings-pitched multiplier effect is worth tracking across future Messick starts.

candidate_rule: Before backing an under that hinges on a specific starter's ERA, cross-check his last 4-6 starts for variance in runs allowed — if the standard deviation is high (e.g., alternating shutdowns and blowups), discount the ERA and widen the projected run range accordingly; N=1 loss, insufficient to establish rule.

candidate_rule: When a returning-from-injury starter's last 2+ starts show a FIP 1.5+ runs above his season ERA, regress projected performance toward the recent sample, not the season line — do not back him as a heavy favorite until he posts at least 3 post-IL starts with sub-4.00 FIP. sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — one start does not confirm a pattern, but the pre-game data signal (4.35 ERA, 5.42 FIP in post-IL starts) was available and underweighted.

candidate_rule: When a starter has a road ERA 2+ runs higher than his home ERA AND a WHIP above 1.25, treat him as a liability for unders regardless of park factors — but validate against a larger sample before hardcoding (N=1 here).
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule. The Sasaki road/home split is a real and trackable signal, but one game is noise — flag for review after 3+ similar setups.

candidate_rule: When a starter's rolling 7-start ERA (4.03) diverges from his season ERA (2.86) by more than 1.00 run, discount the season-long number and re-price the total using the recent-form ERA — N=1 loss on this specific pattern, insufficient to establish a rule.

candidate_rule: When both starters carry BB rates above 8.5%, discount wind-based HR suppression signals by at least 50% on totals, because base-on-balls traffic enables run-scoring without home runs — but N=1, insufficient to establish rule.

candidate_rule: When a starting pitcher's rolling 5-start ERA exceeds 6.50 and his WHIP exceeds 1.90, exclude him from under/low-total constructions regardless of season-long ERA — the regression to mean has already arrived; N=1 here, insufficient to establish rule.

candidate_rule: Before targeting a player prop on an 'elite hitter,' validate current-season K% and two-strike performance — if a hitter's strikeout rate has spiked more than 5 percentage points above career norms, discount the TB Over projection by at least one tier regardless of opposing pitcher profile. sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — one loss does not confirm the pattern, but the underlying data mismatch (career reputation vs. 2026 regression) is worth flagging as a model blind spot to monitor.

candidate_rule: When either starter is returning from the IL with fewer than 2 healthy starts logged, apply a minimum +0.75 run adjustment to the projected total per IL-returning arm — and flag the game for total-side caution regardless of ERA optics; N=1, insufficient to establish rule.

candidate_rule: When anchoring an under on a specific starter, weight the last-6-start ERA and velocity trend equally with the season ERA — if a starter's rolling ERA exceeds his season ERA by 2+ runs AND velocity is down, downgrade the under confidence by at least 1 tier regardless of park factors. candidate_rule: When a pitcher's SwStr% is elite (>15%), do not fade their K prop solely on pitch-count concerns — model expected Ks per batter faced, not just expected innings, and require the opposing lineup's contact rate to be bottom-5 (not merely bottom-10) before treating it as a meaningful suppressor; N=1, insufficient to establish rule.

candidate_rule: Before backing a pitcher-driven under, cross-check the starter's career splits vs. the specific opponent — if ERA vs. that team exceeds 4.50 in 4+ starts, downgrade confidence by at least one tier regardless of season-level home/away splits; N=1, insufficient to establish rule.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — one loss does not confirm the pattern, but the data gap (season home ERA vs. career opponent ERA) is a structural blind spot worth monitoring.

candidate_rule: Before rostering a SP as the primary thesis driver, check his last 2 starts for ER and IP; if he posted 6+ ER in either, downgrade confidence by at least 1 tier regardless of season ERA — but enforce only after N≥10 similar cases to confirm predictive value.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule. One blowout loss following a rough prior start is noise; pattern requires broader sample before codifying.

candidate_rule: Before backing an under anchored by two starters, flag any starter who allowed 6+ ER in their most recent outing as a disqualifying condition — recency of form matters more than season ERA for single-game totals; validate with N≥20 such starts before hardcoding.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient to establish rule — this is a directional signal only, not a validated pattern.
